prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
1) English
Welcome to the team — we’re so glad to have you!
Note: Warm and informal; signals friendliness and a first-name culture, though some workplaces prefer more formal titles at first.

2) Spanish
¡Bienvenido/a al equipo! ¡Encantados de tenerte con nosotros!
Note: Uses an informal, cordial tone (tú) and a gendered form (bienvenido/a); in some countries or with senior staff you'd use formal usted and titles.

3) French
Bienvenue dans l'équipe — ravi(e) de travailler avec vous !
Note: Polite and warm using vous (formal); French workplaces often begin with formal address and may switch to tu later.

4) Mandarin Chinese (Simplified)
很高兴认识你，欢迎加入我们的团队！
Note: Friendly and slightly informal using 你; Chinese workplaces often use family names plus titles and 您 (respectful you) with seniors or to show deference.

5) Arabic
أهلاً وسهلاً بك في فريقنا!
Note: Very warm and hospitable; Arabic greetings are often expressive and respectful, and using titles or honorifics with senior colleagues is common.
